bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very low utility for me and gives no evidence yet of meaningful concessions, so I will anchor on my strongest values across all issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation, and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession toward my preferences. Their latest offer is actually slightly worse for me than their opening offer, so I will make only a small concession from my ideal bid while avoiding repeating my exact previous offer.

I am targeting a high utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent’s offers remain very low for my preferences, with only small movement from all valueF. I avoid repeating my previous bid by making a small concession on issueC while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueD, which are still important to my utility.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid and only making a small concession from my ideal offer. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and show little meaningful concession, so I should hold firm on high-value issues while varying my prior bid slightly to avoid repetition.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid (about 0.792) because it is very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, but their latest bid moved issueC to valueE, so I am testing a concession on issueC while holding firm on my most important issueA and other high-value positions.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while allowing limited movement on issueC, where I have already shown some flexibility. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly anchored on valueF/valueE, with only a small concession on issueC, so I should not make major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.856 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has strongly favored low values for issueB/issueC/issueD but has now conceded issueA to my top value, so I will hold firm on my important issueA and preserve issueB/issueD while making a modest issueC concession. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer history.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small movement on issueB and issueD. Their history suggests they strongly prefer low values on issueC and issueD, so I am conceding somewhat on issueC while holding firm on my most important issueA and high-value issueB/issueD positions.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me, about 0.856 weighted utility, rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers remain mostly low for my preferences, though their latest bid signaled willingness on issueC: valueB, which is also strong for me, so I incorporate that while holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and other valuable issues.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers remain low for me, though the latest bid shows some movement on issueA and issueB; I will continue holding firm on my most important issueA and keep issueB/issueD strong, while offering a moderate concession on issueC.

I am still early in the negotiation at t=0.20, so I targeted a high-utility bid while making a modest concession from my ideal package. The opponent has mostly offered low-value F/E outcomes for me, but their latest bid conceded issueB to my top valueA, so I will hold firm on issueA and issueB while slightly conceding on issueC/issueD to explore a tradeoff.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73, conceding mainly on issueD because the opponent repeatedly proposes low-valued issueD outcomes for me while I preserve my highest-weight issueA and strong issueB. Time is still early-mid at t=0.22, so I should not drop too far, but their offers have slowly improved and suggest some movement. Their latest bid is still much lower for me than my target, so I will make a moderate concession without accepting their pattern wholesale.

At t=0.24 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high utility bid while making limited concessions on lower-cost dimensions. The opponent’s bids remain low for my utility and inconsistent rather than clearly conceding, though they have shown occasional willingness on issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA, so I am holding those while offering flexibility on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.71 because we are only at t=0.26, so I should concede but not rush toward the opponent’s low-utility bids. The opponent has begun conceding on issueA and issueB, but continues to push low values for issueC and issueD, so I am holding firm on high-importance issueA/issueB while offering movement on issueC and especially issueD.

I am targeting a strong but slightly more conciliatory bid around 0.74 utility by holding firm on my most important/high-value issues while conceding issueC to valueF, which the opponent has repeatedly demanded. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet; their history shows some movement on issueA and issueB but a persistent preference for issueC: valueF.

I am targeting a mid-high utility offer around 0.67, conceding more than my earlier near-maximal bids but still protecting my highest-weight issue and keeping issueB strong. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest low-utility package; their recent bids show movement on issueA toward valueB and issueC toward valueC, so I match those while holding issueB at my best value and offering a moderate issueD concession.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, since we are only at t=0.32 and there is no need to drop to the opponent’s latest low-utility offer yet. The opponent has begun conceding on some issues, recently offering issueB/valueB, issueC/valueC, and issueD/valueC, and they have previously shown issueA/valueB and issueC/valueB, so I am keeping high value on issueB while offering a compromise on issueA and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.67, conceding on issueA, issueC, and issueD while holding issueB at my best value because it remains important and the opponent has previously offered issueB: valueA. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s low-utility bids; their offers show gradual movement toward valueB/valueC on several issues, especially issueA and issueD, so I am matching that direction without over-conceding.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids but still well above reservation at t=0.36. The opponent’s offers have improved for me over time, especially with issueB reaching valueA, but they repeatedly ask for issueC: valueF and non-top issueD values, so I concede on issueC and issueD while holding firm on high-importance issueA and issueB. There are no opponent messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.69 while avoiding repeating my exact previous bid; at t=0.38 there is pressure to make meaningful tradeoffs but not to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent has recently signaled willingness on issueA=valueA and issueD=valueC, while often pushing low values on issueC, so I concede on issueC and issueD while holding issueA and issueB where my utility is strongest. There are no natural-language messages to factor in.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.74, conceding on lower/mid-cost issues while preserving my highest-weight issueA and strong issueB. The opponent’s recent offers show movement toward valueA/valueC on issueA, valueA on issueB, and valueD/valueF on issueC/D, so I match their latest issueC=valueD signal and concede issueD to valueC without repeating my previous bid.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66: still clearly above reservation, but with meaningful concessions compared with my earlier high-utility bids. The opponent’s offers have improved from all-low values to combinations including issueA/valueD, issueB/valueC, issueC/valueB, and issueD/valueD, so I am matching some of those signals while holding stronger positions on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting my highest-weight issueA and valuable issueB. The opponent’s latest offer is significantly better for me than their earlier bids, especially by moving to issueA:valueA and issueC:valueD, so I am responding with a nearby counter that mainly asks them to improve issueB while conceding issueD.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding from my previous stronger bid while still protecting my highest-weight issueA reasonably well. The opponent’s latest offer improved to issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, and their recent history shows willingness to offer issueB=valueA and issueC=valueD, so I combine those signals with concessions on issueA and issueD.

At t=0.48 we are in the mid-game, so I target a solid but more realistic utility around 0.65 rather than insisting on the maximum. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, especially with their latest bid using issueA=valueB and issueC=valueB, so I keep those concessions while asking for issueB=valueA, which they have offered before and is valuable to me.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.63, conceding enough to improve acceptance chances without dropping near reservation. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me over time, and they already proposed this exact valid bid at t=0.44, so it is a credible compromise while avoiding repeating my previous bid.
